title: 3 Dots Loader
description: Three-dot gooey loader using an SVG feColorMatrix filter. Each dot follows a separate CSS keyframe path, then they converge and rotate. Pure CSS animation with no JavaScript.
prompt_text:
# 3 Dots Loader Three-dot gooey loader using an SVG feColorMatrix filter. Each dot follows a separate CSS keyframe path, then they converge and rotate. Pure CSS animation with no JavaScript. ## Use when - Showing the user that data is being fetched and they should wait. - Indicating a system thinking or processing state with an organic, gooey animation. - Displaying a first-load indicator before content is ready. ## Stack - Framework: react - Libraries: none - Requires: tailwind ## Install ```bash none ``` ## Component — 3-dots-loader.tsx ```tsx import { cn } from "@/lib/utils"; import { useState } from "react"; export const Component = () => { const [count, setCount] = useState(0); return ( <>
); }; ``` ## Demo ```tsx import { Component } from "@/components/ui/3-dots-loader"; export default function DemoOne() { return ; } ``` ## Notes - Code bug: uses `class=` instead of `className=` in JSX (lines with `
` etc.). This will cause React warnings. Copy verbatim per protocol; fix manually before production use. - Requires CSS class definitions (`.container`, `.dot`, `.dot-1`, `.dot-2`, `.dot-3`) and the listed keyframes to be added to your global stylesheet.
